course.title: CompTIA A+ V15 Applied IT Support and Troubleshooting
course.description: Beginning IT support learners who need a complete, safe, evidence-driven path from first support interaction through current CompTIA A+ V15 Core 1 and Core 2 coverage and independently defended troubleshooting practice. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate every unique objective in current comptia a+ v15 core 1 220-1201 and core 2 220-1202, plus the official troubleshooting methodology competency standard taught before technical changes through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate cumulative support practice across hardware, mobile, printers, networking, virtualization, cloud, windows, macos, linux, chromeos, android, applications, security, incident response, operations, communication, accessibility, privacy, sustainability, and performance-task defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate two hundred observable competencies, six materially different stakeholder projects, misconception-targeted theory, guided workshops, faded debugging, changed-device labs, delayed retrieval, scenario quizzes, and one unfamiliar cumulative examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate original deterministic support-evidence manifests that make authorization, safety, baseline, hypothesis, test, observation, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer limits mechanically reviewable through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves user impact, asset and environment identity, authorization, safety, baseline, recent change, privacy, accessibility, least-destructive testing, evidence provenance, rollback, verification, documentation, escalation, and transfer boundaries before adding complexity.

Fictional browser cases never execute commands, inspect the host, connect to devices or networks, access accounts, change configuration, handle malware, sanitize media, open equipment, contact people, or imply authorization for real work.

Passing work requires a prediction, bounded discriminating test, observed result, changed case, rejected plausible failure, causal repair, side-effect check, prevention or recurrence plan, accessible user communication, and explicit remaining uncertainty.

Windows, Apple, Android, Linux distribution, firmware, wireless, USB, cloud, security, recycling, licensing, privacy, incident, and exam details can change; learners must name the version, date, jurisdiction, owner, and authoritative source for consequential transfer decisions.
Exam objective coverage sets minimum breadth, not maximum competence. Learners must connect each fact to a safe support decision and observable mastery evidence rather than memorize labels alone.

credential.title: CompTIA A+ V15 Applied IT Support and Troubleshooting Certificate

modules.title: Support Evidence, Authorization, and Troubleshooting Method
modules.description: A new technician receives a vague intermittent-failure ticket and pressure to change the device before confirming authority, impact, baseline, recent changes, or a recovery path. Learners cumulatively produce causal support incident record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate user goals, technician authority, system ownership, policy, safety, privacy, and escalation before touching state.
Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement, verify, prevent, and document as an evidence loop rather than a memorized script.
Record expected behavior, observed behavior, user impact, scope, frequency, severity, and a reproducible baseline without blaming the user.

modules.title: Electrical, ESD, Battery, Fire, Lifting, and Workspace Safety
modules.description: A repair clinic has a swollen battery, a heavy printer, an energized power supply, loose jewelry, poor ventilation, and no documented emergency or disposal route. Learners cumulatively produce safe work authorization and hazard-control plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Survey electrical, thermal, chemical, mechanical, trip, ergonomic, fire, and data hazards before selecting controls.
Match grounding, antistatic bags, mats, humidity, handling, and packaging controls to static-sensitive work.
Recognize energized, capacitor, liquid, damaged-battery, odor, heat, and deformation stop conditions and route them to qualified handling.

modules.title: Device Requirements, Compatibility, Serviceability, and Asset Baselines
modules.description: A nonprofit requests inexpensive upgrades for mixed devices, but application requirements, ports, power, firmware, warranties, accessibility needs, lifecycle, and asset history conflict. Learners cumulatively produce requirements-to-compatible-change matrix through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate functional, performance, capacity, interface, security, accessibility, support, environmental, cost, and lifecycle requirements.
Interpret decimal and binary capacity, frequency, transfer rate, latency, dimensions, connector markings, model identifiers, and tolerance without false precision.
Trace physical fit, electrical limits, protocol support, firmware, drivers, operating system, application, policy, and warranty as one compatibility chain.

modules.title: Displays, Cameras, Audio, and Accessible Output
modules.description: A hybrid classroom has unreadable text, intermittent external video, echo, camera framing failures, color-only status, and conflicting accessibility and privacy needs. Learners cumulatively produce accessible audiovisual signal-path diagnosis through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compare LCD panel types, OLED, projectors, digitizers, backlights, inverters, touch layers, privacy filters, and failure signatures.
Relate physical size, pixel resolution, density, scaling, refresh, orientation, reflow, zoom, and readable workspace to the user task.
Trace source, GPU, port, cable, adapter, dock, sink, input selection, negotiation, power, and operating-system state before replacing parts.

modules.title: Cables, Connectors, Adapters, Interfaces, and Capability Negotiation
modules.description: A mobile workstation uses look-alike USB-C cables, a dock, legacy adapters, Ethernet, external storage, and power delivery, but only some combinations carry the required data, video, and power. objectives: Identify common USB, video, storage, network, audio, power, serial, and legacy connector form factors without inferring capability from shape alone.
